member.office_address: 2233 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-2103

form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
Instructions: Please fill out one form per request. If you would like to submit a FY27 NDAA or Defense Appropriations request, please fill out this form instead: https://forms.gle/kXrKzFzVj6Ukbvvf7 

We have not yet received updated CPF guidance from the Appropriations Committee. Please DO NOT include any CPF requests at this time. An additional form will be sent out when Committee released guidance.

To the extent possible, please provide detailed information regarding the location of your request in the subcommittee bill or report, down to the activity, account, or country level (if applicable).

fields.label: First Name
Last Name
Phone Number
Organization Name
City
State
Zip Code
Is your organization located in or operates primarily in Massachusetts?
Is your organization located in or operates primarily out of the 3rd District of Massachusetts?
Please explain your MA-03 connection (facility locations, programming in the District, constituents are employees etc.)
Organization's Contact / Representation in DC - Name
Organization's Contact / Representation in DC - Email
Organization's Contact / Representation in DC - Phone

If this is the only request you are submitting, check "#1." If you are submitting multiple requests, please rank this submission in order of priority (please limit submissions to no more than 5 appropriation requests)
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is specific to the individual submitter's full slate of requests and cannot be determined from the provided context.

fields.label: Please list other pertinent Members you are submitting this request to (e.g. Rep. Neal due to facility in MA-01)
fields.rationale: This field requires knowledge of the submitter's specific submission strategy and other congressional offices they intend to contact, which is not provided in the context.

fields.label: Select the Appropriations Bill
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education
fields.rationale: NASA funding falls under the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee. The option 'Commerce, Science, Justice' is the closest match to the CJS subcommittee.

fields.label: Request Type
fields.rationale: The request context explicitly identifies this as a programmatic funding request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Name of Department or Independent Agency
fields.rationale: Matches the requested agency in the reference values.

fields.label: Name of Agency or Bureau within Department (if applicable)
fields.rationale: Matches the specific bureau/account name provided in context.

fields.label: Name of Account
fields.rationale: Standard NASA account name from reference values.

fields.label: Name of Program
fields.rationale: Consistent with the programmatic request for SMD topline funding.

fields.label: Describe the reason for the request and provide any available economic impact information - 100 words maximum.
fields.draft_value: This $9 billion request for NASA Science (SMD) ensures U.S. leadership in space against rising international competition. SMD funding supports high-skill jobs and innovation in MA-03, where institutions like UMass Lowell and companies like Aerodyne Research Inc. have received over $10.7M in recent years for critical work in Earth observation and coronagraph hardware. Every $1 invested in NASA returns $3 to the economy. Robust funding maintains our domestic talent pipeline and technical workforce, which was significantly disrupted by recent budget volatility, ensuring the U.S. remains the global leader in discovery.
fields.rationale: Combines local economic data ($10.7M in MA-03), specific local recipients (UMass Lowell, Aerodyne), and strategic advocacy points regarding workforce stability and competition with China.

fields.label: FY 26 House Funding
fields.help_text: Provide the funding level in the FY 2026 House-passed bill
fields.rationale: Uses the pre-filled/authoritative enacted value for FY2026.

fields.label: FY26 Senate Funding
fields.help_text: Provide the funding level in the FY 2026 Senate-passed bill
fields.rationale: Uses the pre-filled/authoritative enacted value for FY2026.

fields.label: FY26 Conferenced Funding Level
fields.help_text: Provide the enacted level or final conferenced amount for FY 2026
fields.rationale: Uses the pre-filled/authoritative enacted value for FY2026.

fields.label: FY27 President's Budget Request Level (Write N/A if unavailable)
fields.rationale: Reference values indicate the President's Budget Request has not been released yet.

fields.label: Funding Level Requested
options: Maintain FY26 House Enacted Funding Level
Maintain FY26 Senate Enacted Funding Level
fields.rationale: The requested amount of $9,000,000,000 is an increase over the FY2026 enacted level of $7,250,000,000 and does not match the other specific categorical options provided.

fields.label: Name of Department or Independent Agency
fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Bill Language requests; the request context states Bill Language is N/A.

fields.label: Name of Bureau or Office within Department/Agency
fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Bill Language requests; the request context states Bill Language is N/A.

fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Bill Language requests; the request context states Bill Language is N/A.

fields.label: Name of Program (N/A if not applicable)
fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Bill Language requests; the request context states Bill Language is N/A.

fields.label: Please summarize the bill language request. Describe the reason for the request and provide any available economic impact information - 100 words maximum.
fields.rationale: The request is programmatic, and the provided context specifies that no bill language is being requested (N/A). Under the 'Mutually Exclusive Sections' rule, this field does not apply to the current request type.

fields.label: Please provide requested bill language
fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Bill Language requests; the request context states Bill Language is N/A.

fields.label: Name of Department or Independent Agency
fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Report Language requests; the request context states Report Language is N/A.

fields.label: Name of Bureau or Office within Department/Agency
fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Report Language requests; the request context states Report Language is N/A.

fields.label: Name of Account (N/A if not applicable)
fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Report Language requests; the request context states Report Language is N/A.

fields.label: Name of Program (N/A if not applicable)
fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Report Language requests; the request context states Report Language is N/A.

fields.label: Please summarize report language request. Describe the reason for the request and provide any available economic impact information - 100 words maximum.
fields.rationale: The request is programmatic, and the provided context specifies that no report language is being requested (N/A). Under the 'Mutually Exclusive Sections' rule, this field does not apply to the current request type.

fields.label: Please provide requested Report Language
fields.rationale: This field is part of a section for Report Language requests; the request context states Report Language is N/A.

fields.label: Funding Amount for FY27 (dollar amount)
fields.rationale: Uses the exact funding ask amount provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Acknowledgement
fields.rationale: Standard confirmation of provided information.

top_contracts.description: Phase ii SBIR 80nssc25c0046 airborne spectroscopic static temperature sensor
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing an advanced airborne static temperature sensor.

top_contracts.description: Maintenance & supposrt for goddard owned oxford instrument equipment in code
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Maintaining specialized scientific instruments for NASA Goddard operations.

top_grants.description: Background: in support of NASA'S Earth Surface and Interior (ESI) focus area  the Terrestrial  Reference Frame (TRF) is the foundation for virtually all airborne  space-based  and ground-based Earth observations. This Frame  is developed by combining the observations from Satellite Laser Ranging (SLR)  Very Long Baseline  Interferometry (VLBI)  the Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS)  and Doppler Orbitography and  Radiopositioning Integrated by Satellite (DORIS) stations  and is realized as an International  standard through the International Terrestrial Reference Frame (ITRF). Combining the different  measurement techniques is only possible with accurate knowledge of the relative measurement  Reference points between co-located systems  and doing so is essential to take full advantage of  the strengths of each technique. Currently  the itrf is limited in accuracy by systematic errors in  tying together the contributions from the different geodetic techniques. Since standard  ground-based surveys providing ties between geodetic techniques have reached the limit of their  capabilities  a new approach is required that extends technique ties into space.  Major goals: we propose to develop a new space flight instrument and verify a measurement concept  that enables the determination of systematic errors between the vlbi  gnss  and slr independent  measurement techniques by extending surveying techniques out to spaceflight assets. The proposed  instrument  functioning as a gnss l-to-x-band transponder  establishes frequency compatibility  between vlbi and gnss  thereby facilitating a direct space-based geodetic tie between these two  radio-based techniques in post- processing. Separate Laser retro-reflectors flown concurrently with  gritss would provide additional connection to the slr network. Because the measurement concept by  which the vlbi/gnss systematic errors will be determined only requires one vlbi station to observe  the space vehicle  the space vehicle may be in low-earth orbit. This is advantageous as it opens up  the possibility of using inexpensive cubesats or other small satellites  making it possible to  implement a cost-effective constellation of spacecraft to provide better Global coverage and  further improve the accuracy of the geodetic site ties.  Specific tasks:  1) develop modified version of an existing space flight gnss receiver to support gnss relaying  functionality procure commercial space flight uhf (low data rate)  S  and x-band (high data rate)  digital data transmitters.  2) design and fabricate a fit-form-function instrument capable of being implemented within a  cubesat/small Satellite.  3) bench test breadboard concept of gnss-to-vlbi transponder with gnss synthetic signal generators  and space vehicle gnss receivers.  4) bench test breadboard concept of gnss-to-vlbi transponder with gnss synthetic signal generators  and space vehicle gnss receivers.  5) on sight ground test of gnss-to-vlbi transponder at goddard geophysical astronomical  observatory. 6) airborne flight test evaluation of gnss-to-vlbi transponder at goddard geophysical astronomical  observatory.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing CubeSat hardware to enhance global Earth observation accuracy.

top_grants.description: 21-apra21-0055  picture-d: planetary imaging coronagraph testbed using a reusable experiment for debris disks.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing advanced coronagraph hardware for imaging distant debris disks.

top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress.   Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond.   Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines   develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Executing balanced science supporting exploration, space weather, and planetary defense.

top_grants.description: The roses funding opportunity goals are: execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress. Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond. Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Executing strategic science supporting exploration, Earth applications, and planetary defense.

top_grants.description: e014042-using glacial moraine sequences on arsia and pavonis mons to constrain recent climate change events on Mars by employingan ice sheet model.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Modeling Martian glacial deposits to determine recent climate history.

Top Active Awards.Description: Background: in support of NASA'S Earth Surface and Interior (ESI) focus area  the Terrestrial  Reference Frame (TRF) is the foundation for virtually all airborne  space-based  and ground-based Earth observations. This Frame  is developed by combining the observations from Satellite Laser Ranging (SLR)  Very Long Baseline  Interferometry (VLBI)  the Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS)  and Doppler Orbitography and  Radiopositioning Integrated by Satellite (DORIS) stations  and is realized as an International  standard through the International Terrestrial Reference Frame (ITRF).
